title: Tender Earthy Japanese Smoked Mackerel Bowl
description: A Japanese-inspired meal prep dish featuring tender smoked mackerel, earthy vegetables, and a nutty brown rice bowl. Perfect for a quick and easy breakfast, lunch, or snack.
instructions: Rinse the brown rice in a fine-mesh strainer and cook according to package instructions.
In a small saucepan, toast the sesame seeds over low heat, stirring frequently, until lightly browned.
In a separate saucepan, whisk together the soy sauce, sake, and mirin. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Arrange the smoked mackerel fillets on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Drizzle with a little water and dot with a little oil. Bake for 10-15 minutes, or until warm and flaky.
While the mackerel is baking, prepare a bed of brown rice and arrange on a plate or bowl.
Add the toasted sesame seeds, shredded daikon, and pickled ginger on top of the rice.
Remove the mackerel from the oven and place on top of the rice.
Drizzle the warmed soy sauce mixture over the mackerel and garnish with chopped green onions.
Serve immediately, or refrigerate for up to 5 days and reheat as needed.
Store the leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Freeze the leftovers for up to 2 months, thawing as needed.
